sections.title: The King's Little Dumpling
Talking to King Bolren
Talking to Bolren again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again, Bolren.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Did you have a look down in the caves?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet. I'll head down there now and see if Golrie can give me a hand.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again, Bolren.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Hello there. It's good to see you again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see you have a new pet.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Ah, you've met Dumpling.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dumpling? I suppose it looks a little bit like a dumpling...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An evil six-legged dumpling!

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Hey! Dumpling isn't evil. She has cute ears and a little bobbly tail!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look at it! It's clearly evil. It has six legs!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And those eyes... those black, black eyes... If you stare into them, you can see into its black soul!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahem, anyway, that's not how I know this creature is evil. I've seen one before, and I'm afraid I have some bad news.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I came across some creatures like Dumpling at the Gnome Stronghold. They're evil things created using dark magic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If this is anything like the situation at the Gnome Stronghold, then Dumpling was sent by Glouphrie to wheedle itself into your affections and spy on you.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: You can't be serious!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid so. The creature is shrouded by illusion magic to make it look like a cute pet, but in reality it's this horrible... black spiky thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's watching you all the time, sending details of all your secret dealings back to Glouphrie.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: You've been listening to too many gnomish children's stories! Glouphrie hasn't been seen since before I was King. He's almost certainly long-dead by now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, what if I were to prove it to you? I unmasked the creatures at the Gnome Stronghold using a machine created by Oaknock the Engineer. Maybe I could do it again.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: It's going to need to be very solid proof for me to just cast Dumpling aside. She's become my constant companion!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh it will be solid. I just need an anti-illusion device... I don't suppose you have one of those around here?

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: I don't think so. What does one look like?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A big machine made out of wood and crystal.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Crystal? As in elven crystal? I haven't seen any elven crystal around here since...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Since?

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Since Yewnock came to visit!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yewnock?

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Oaknock's son, of course! He visited a long time ago, before I was even King. I remember he brought all sorts of strange things with him, including some elven crystals!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So perhaps there's a device nearby. When he was here, where did Yewnock spend a lot of his time?

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: He did a lot of work down in the caves. I think he liked how nice and quiet it was down there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It sounds like I need to pay Golrie a visit. I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... if you're sure.

sections.title: Talking to Golrie
Talking to Golrie again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Golrie. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Better now I'm on the right side of these bars.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help with a device.

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Did you check the storeroom? You can get to it through the small tunnel in the other chamber.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll take a look. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Golrie. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Better now I'm on the right side of these bars.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help with a device.

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: A device?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. One made using elven crystal.

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Ah, well I've got plenty of elven stuff. It's all lying around here somewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you have so much? Are you some sort of collector?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Not me, my grandfather. At least, he used to be. I'm not sure whether he's still alive or not. He hasn't sent me any souvenirs for a while now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Souvenirs?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: That's right. He was a funny gnome, old gramps. He got into a bit of... trouble a while back and couldn't really stick around. He ended up travelling the world collecting elven artefacts after that.

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: It used to be a monthly occurence. A terrorbird would turn up with a big parcel and I'd throw it in with the other stuff in the storeroom. It's a bit of a mess now in there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Storeroom? Could I take a look?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Oh, certainly. I should really sort through all of his things at some point. There's probably some quite cool stuff in there.

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Have a look in the other chamber and you'll find a small tunnel into the storeroom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

sections.title: Talking to King Bolren after talking to Golrie

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again, Bolren.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Did you have a look down in the caves?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it. I'll let you know once I've made some progress.

sections.title: Yewnock's Legacy
Bolrie's Diary
Chapter 1. Bad advice

lines.speaker: Bolrie
lines.text: When I became King of the Tree Gnome Village, I thought I was lucky, for I did so during a time of peace. However, it seemed that peace was not to last.

lines.speaker: Bolrie
lines.text: Human activity in the area was increasing, and conflict between our two races seemed inevitable.

lines.speaker: Bolrie
lines.text: Fearing the worst, I had my trusted advisor reach out to King Healthorg of the Gnome Stronghold, but his response left much to be desired...

lines.speaker: Advisor
lines.text: My King, we have received word from King Healthorg.

lines.speaker: King Bolrie
lines.text: Finally! How long before we can expect his support?

lines.speaker: Advisor
lines.text: He will not be sending any support, my King. He does not wish to start a conflict with the humans.

lines.speaker: King Bolrie
lines.text: What? But can't he see what will happen if we don't act now! The humans will destroy us!

lines.speaker: Advisor
lines.text: I have conversed with the spirit trees, my King, and they are in full agreement. If we do not act, Khazard and his forces will. It is not a question of if, but when.

lines.speaker: King Bolrie
lines.text: Healthorg has become a weak old fool! He's blind to our problems.

lines.speaker: Advisor
lines.text: All may not be lost. I hear his illness is progressing. Soon, the Gnome Stronghold will need to pick a new ruler.

lines.speaker: King Bolrie
lines.text: They'll almost certainly pick his son, but Argenthorg is just as bad as his father. If he becomes King, he will only appease the humans!

lines.speaker: Advisor
lines.text: Then perhaps they need presenting with an alternate option.

lines.speaker: King Bolrie
lines.text: Yes... perhaps they do.

sections.title: Chapter 2. The King is dead

lines.speaker: Bolrie
lines.text: And so the reign of the most famous gnome king came to an end.

lines.speaker: Bolrie
lines.text: With Healthorg gone, voting on his replacement would soon begin. Many expected his son, Argenthorg, to be the only serious contender.

lines.speaker: Bolrie
lines.text: But my advisor and I had other ideas...

sections.title: Chapter 3. Eyes opened

lines.speaker: Bolrie
lines.text: Despite our best attempts, Argenthorg looked certain to win the election.

lines.speaker: Bolrie
lines.text: Healthorg was a great king far longer than he was a weak one. Everyone assumed his son would be as great as he was.

lines.speaker: Bolrie
lines.text: We needed to show them the truth. We needed to show them that Argenthorg was weak. My advisor said he knew how...

lines.speaker: Advisor
lines.text: Argenthorg... this is human food!

lines.speaker: King Bolrie
lines.text: So it's true! A gnome who loves humans more than his own kind!

lines.speaker: Argenthorg
lines.text: I don't understand! What is this?

lines.speaker: King Bolrie
lines.text: The truth is out now, Argenthorg. You are not fit to be a king!

lines.speaker: Argenthorg
lines.text: This is all a lie! I've been framed!

lines.speaker: Advisor
lines.text: The evidence is right in front of us, Argenthorg.

lines.speaker: Argenthorg
lines.text: Wait... I know what this is! Father warned me about this!

lines.speaker: King Bolrie
lines.text: What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Argenthorg
lines.text: Guards! Seek out Yewnock at once. Tell him to switch the machine on!

lines.speaker: Argenthorg
lines.text: Glouphrie!

lines.speaker: King Bolrie
lines.text: No! I don't believe it. I trusted you!

lines.speaker: Glouphrie the Untrusted
lines.text: As you should have. I sought only to strengthen the Gnomish Kingdoms, just like you did.

lines.speaker: King Bolrie
lines.text: Lier! You deceived me!

lines.speaker: Glouphrie the Untrusted
lines.text: No, I simply told you what you wanted to hear, Bolrie. Never forget that...

lines.speaker: Bolrie
lines.text: There ended my campaign to become the new king of the Gnome Stronghold. Argenthorg was crowned soon after and Glouphrie disappeared once more.

lines.speaker: Bolrie
lines.text: I don't know what the gnomes of the Tree Gnome Village will think once they hear what I've done, but it does not matter. They deserve a better ruler than I.

lines.speaker: Bolrie
lines.text: And so I am preparing to leave the lands of my people, but before I go, I will ensure foul illusions cannot be allowed to shame us gnomes again.

lines.speaker: Bolrie
lines.text: As for the one who chooses to practise such dark arts... He will be punished. I will make it my life's purpose...

sections.title: Talking to Golrie after activating Yewnock's machine

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Golrie. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Better now I'm on the right side of these bars.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I managed to find that device.

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Wonderful! Though you know, I don't think you ever actually mentioned what the device does.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It counteracts illusion magic in the surrounding area. This one should cover the whole of the Tree Gnome Village. It might be useful for someone to remember that in case it's needed again.

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Oh, well I'll be sure to keep that in mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found Bolrie's diary. Do you know anything about it?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Of course! Bolrie is my grandfather... assuming he's still alive, at least.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Bolrie... Golrie... I should have guessed. You seem to like giving your children similar names, just to be confusing.

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Certainly, in our family that's true. King Bolren is also Bolrie's younger brother, in case you didn't know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So that means you're King Bolren's grand-nephew, right?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does that mean that you're in line for the throne?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Gnomes don't have a hereditary monarchy. We have a democratic monarchy. We vote for our kings.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, that's unusual. So you've never thought about being the King?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: That might be fun. I wouldn't want to be like gramps though. He liked wars a bit too much. I do sometimes discuss the Khazard War with the hobgobbos, but they don't have a particularly sharp political sense.

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Personally, I don't think we should fight his troops head- on. We're smarter than that. We should come up with some ingenious method of defeating Khazard, like they did at the Battle of Atarisundri!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're very well informed for a gnome in a cave. Most gnomes I've talked to have forgotten most of their own history.

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Gnomes don't tend to dwell on the past, but I suppose I'm not a typical gnome. I get lots of time to think and read in here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So have you heard from Bolrie recently?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: No, the parcels stopped arriving a few years ago. I guess he's either found what he was looking for, or has died in the attempt.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was he looking for?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: I'm not sure. He was definitely obsessed with finding out as much as he could about elven magic. He also seemed to have a bit of a grudge against Glouphrie.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe he's looking for Glouphrie.

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Who knows, eh? Who knows?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where did that crystal bowl come from?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: A huge battle tortoise arrived a while back carrying a crate with it in. Sent by gramps, of course. It looks pretty, doesn't it? Would probably look great with some plants in it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think it's meant to be used as a plant pot.

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Well, what's it for then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a singing bowl. You can use it to shape elven crystal.

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Ohh... How exciting!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: See you later.

sections.title: Talking to Golrie after telling him you found the device

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Golrie. How's it going?

lines.speaker: Golrie
lines.text: Better now I'm on the right side of these bars.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have a question about your storeroom.

sections.title: Talking to King Bolren after activating Yewnock's machine

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Well, it sadly seems you were right. Please get rid of the nasty creature. Then we can talk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.

sections.title: Talking to King Bolren after killing the evil creature
Talking to King Bolren again

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Hello there. Any luck finding Longramble?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: I'm sure Aluft Gianne jnr. can help. You'll find him in his restaurant at the Grand Tree. Good luck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Thank you for dealing with that... thing. I apologise for not believing you before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's not a problem.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: While I am glad the matter has been sorted, I must admit that this situation has me quite concerned. You said that there were many of these creatures posted all around the Gnome Stronghold?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. There were six.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Why did Glouphrie send so many there, but only one here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe he thought it was more important to spy on the Gnome Stronghold and Narnode.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: That's outrageous! The Gnome Stronghold is not six times more important than our village here! He should have sent at least four watchers, maybe five!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you want more evil watchers?

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Well, yes. I mean no, of course not. Still, Glouphrie has a lot to answer for! If I ever get my hands on him I'll... er... I'll give him a good talking to!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... Well, maybe we should start by trying to find him.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Yes, of course! Still, that won't be an easy feat. It is said that after Glouphrie was driven out of our lands, he founded the hidden city of Arposandra. To find him, we must first find Arposandra!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And nobody knows where Arposandra is. Not a great start.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Indeed. Many a gnome has tried and failed to find the city. Some, like Longramble, are still out there searching for it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Longramble?

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Yes, one of my citizens. Longramble is a bit of an explorer. He's been out there searching for Arposandra for some time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Did he offer any suggestion of where he thought it might be?

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: He said that he was sure Arposandra was somewhere between here and the edge of the world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... that doesn't really help.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: No, I suppose not. Still, if you want to find Arposandra, finding Longramble might not be a bad place to start.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But how would I find him?

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: I'm not sure, but I bet Aluft Gianne jnr. could help. His restaurant can supposedly deliver food to any gnome, no matter where they are.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like the best option then. We just have to hope Longramble didn't end up finding the edge of the world and falling off.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: You'll find Aluft Gianne jnr. in his restaurant at the Grand Tree. Good luck!

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Oh, and if you find Glouphrie, tell him... tell him... I'm very angry with him!

sections.title: Talking to Gianne jnr.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help finding a certain gnome.

lines.speaker: Gianne jnr.
lines.text: Hmm, that's sensitive information you know. Who is this gnome?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm looking for Longramble, the explorer.

lines.speaker: Gianne jnr.
lines.text: Ah, well, that might be different. I have a long-standing order for Longramble. Problem is, no one has been able to reach him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you know where he is?

lines.speaker: Gianne jnr.
lines.text: Oh yes! I have his coordinates, but I can't find them on my Doogle Maps. Two degrees, seven minutes south. Three degrees, seven minutes west.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Doogle Maps?

lines.speaker: Gianne jnr.
lines.text: Of course! My maps are all on dried doogle leaves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I guess I'd better get hunting. Sounds like I'll be needing a sextant, watch and chart.

lines.speaker: Gianne jnr.
lines.text: Oh, if you do find Longramble, I'd appreciate it if you could deliver his order to him. He's requested some tasty tangled toad's legs.

sections.title: Talking to Gianne jnr. again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you remind me of Longramble's coordinates?

lines.speaker: Gianne jnr.
lines.text: Two degrees, seven minutes south. Three degrees, seven minutes west.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I guess I'd better get hunting. Sounds like I'll be needing a sextant, watch and chart.

lines.speaker: Gianne jnr.
lines.text: If you do find Longramble, I'd appreciate it if you could deliver his order to him. He's requested some tasty tangled toad's legs.

sections.title: Arbor Incomitata
Talking to the Spirit Tree before talking to Longramble
Enter sewer entrance before talking to Longramble
Talking to Longramble
Talking to Longramble again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again. Where did you say that outlet was?

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: It's just to the west of here. Shouldn't be too hard to find.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mr Longramble, I presume?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Trouble speaking? You're not one of those gnomes like Hazelmere that only speaks in ancient gnomish, are you?

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: No... It's just... It's been so long! All this time with only myself for company!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it looks like you have some new company now. I'm [player name], and I've been looking for you.

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm hoping you've found something that I'm also looking for.

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: Do you mean the edge of the world? It's been my life's ambition to find it and be the first gnome to stare over its edge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Edge? Are you sure there's an edge? I've heard it goes on forever.

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: Everything has an edge. Eventually I'll find it. I'll keep heading westwards until I get there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If the world is spherical though, you'll never find the edge.

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: Spherical? Don't be silly! The world is clearly flat. If it was ball-shaped, we'd all roll off the side. Silly human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe the world is round, but there's some magical force that makes us stick to the ground.

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: Now you're just making fun of me. I don't think you realise the gravity of the situation!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... Anyway, that's not why I'm here. I thought you were looking for the hidden city of Arposandra?

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: That is true, but I'm only doing that as a detour from my journey to the edge of the world. I figured it would be on the way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And have you had any luck?

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: I think so. There's a waste outlet nearby that I believe may be coming from the city of Arposandra!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What makes you think that?

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: Well, have a look for yourself. There's a huge swamp of nasty goo out west that definitely isn't natural. It's coming from that outlet.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't mean it's coming from Arposandra though.

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: Indeed, but there is one other thing. The outlet appears to be patrolled by a creature that suggests gnomish involvement... A giant tortoise!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, that sounds a lot more promising.

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: Exactly! The thing is, there seems to be something wrong with it. It seems... unwell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: In what way?

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: It has some large tentacles growing from underneath its shell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tentacles? That's more than unwell, that's... warped!

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: Well, whatever you call it, it's not normal. Maybe it's an illness caused by Glouphrie's magic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm, I'd better have a look myself.

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: Go for it. The outlet isn't hard to find. It's just to the west.

sections.title: Talking to King Bolren after talking to Longramble

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Hello there. How goes the quest for Arposandra?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think it's going well. I found Longramble, and he was able to point me in the right direction.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Glad to hear it.

sections.title: Walking towards the spirit tree
Talking to the spirit tree after talking to Longramble
Talking to the spirit tree again

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: I sense that you haven't yet managed to cleanse the tree? What is holding you up?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How am I meant to do it?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: You'll need to go and fetch a crystal chime.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Just use your crystal chime.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll give it a go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... hello... Was that you calling me?

lines.speaker: Spirit Tree
lines.text: The tree whisperer has come to my aid. Thank Guthix.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have a name?

lines.speaker: Spirit Tree
lines.text: You may call me Incomitatus, for I am alone and friendless. You are not a small person of Guthix. How do you speak with me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... I don't know. Can't anyone just speak to you?

lines.speaker: Spirit Tree
lines.text: Only those who have opened their minds to the spirits of the earth can talk with spirit trees. You surely must have been trained by a great friend to us, as this is a gift not easily come by.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, it's true that I have spoken to the odd tree in the past...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, wait a minute, this reminds me of something. Hazelmere did something like this to me a while ago. You know, talking using the mind.

lines.speaker: Spirit Tree
lines.text: The one you call Hazelmere... he is a friend of trees. He would be able to help me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I could help in some way. What exactly is wrong with you?

lines.speaker: Spirit Tree
lines.text: I have lost my connection to the Anima Mundi. The earth has corrupted my roots and they find only black ichor. My xylem and phloem are tainted. I am alone.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... I'm sure there's a way to fix this. What would Hazelmere do?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: What? What do you want? You shouldn't disturb me like that. I was deep in contemplation. Looking into the future is not something you can do on a whim, you know!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hazelmere?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Yes of course! You did call for me, didn't you? And what did I tell you about flapping your lips during a mind meld? Use your brain!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, sorry.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Now, this had best be important. I was in the middle of a dream, a vision of the future. There was something I was meant to see...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's good to hear you're still as grumpy as always.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Hmph! How did you manage to perform a mind meld from so far away anyway? Such skills are far beyond a human. Unless...

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Of course, a spirit tree. I sense this one to be rather unwell. What happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was hoping you could tell me.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Alright, let me try to sense the problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hazelmere?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Help me!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Right, all done. Ah, and it seems you haven't suffered any permanent damage. That's fortunate.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fortunate? You didn't warn me of any risk before giving me that nightmare!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: You have a strong spirit. I assumed you'd be alright. And, after all, it's the spirit tree that really matters. You'll only live for a handful of decades. The tree should live for centuries.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not sure I approve of your low regard for my welfare.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: The welfare of the tree is our concern right now. Something has poisoned the soil, blocking the tree's connection with the Anima Mundi. This is worrying in itself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is that?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Well, no ordinary poison could do this. Only by separating the Anima Mundi from its source...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry, you're confusing me.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: I forget I'm dealing with a human. I'll spell it out nice and clearly for you. The Anima Mundi is created by all natural things. It is their soul, and a sort of natural energy.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: It is powerful and dangerous, and only by the balance of Guthix does it remain in check. Creatures live, creatures die. The balance is maintained.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how has this caused Incomitatus to become ill?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Powerful magics could be used to separate the Anima Mundi from its source. This would produce a great deal of power, power that could be used in an unbridled way.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: However, doing so would unbalance the whole pattern of life. The ichor in the soil appears to be the unnatural waste product of such tinkerings.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There is a huge evil-smelling swamp of tar just to the west of here. Is that related?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Most likely, but to make enough of this ichor to create a swamp would take an enormous amount of work and would create a dizzying amount of energy.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: It would be an abomination, to plunder the earth in such a massive way. Who would need to do this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glouphrie?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Hmph, maybe. Anyway, our immediate problem is to help our spirit brethren.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there some sort of antidote I could give to the tree?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: If you had been paying attention, you'd remember that I said this is no normal poison. Some sort of spiritual cleansing will be needed in order to return the spirit tree's connection to the Anima Mundi.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: I do not have the power to do this kind of thing. This will require the magic of Seren and her elves. A pure note, created by elven crystal, would have a purifying effect on anything nearby.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Luckily, you seem to already be carrying the answer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you know what I'm carrying?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, mind meld. Of course.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Use your crystal chime to try and cleanse the tree. Let us hope this works.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll give it a go.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Luckily, it seems you already encountered the answer below the Tree Gnome Village.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do you know what I've encountered?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, mind meld. Of course.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Bring a crystal chime here and use it to try and cleanse the tree.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll be back soon.

sections.title: Talking to Longramble after talking to the spirit tree

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know there's quite an ill-looking spirit tree over there, right?

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: Oh yes, I forgot about that. Poor thing isn't looking great, but I'm not sure what can be done. Trees aren't really my area of expertise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm...

sections.title: Talking to Hazelmere at his house after talking to the spirit tree

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: What are you doing here? That spirit tree needs our help. Get back there!

sections.title: Using the crystal chime on the spirit tree

lines.speaker: Spirit Tree
lines.text: Thank you, tree whisperer. I can feel the Anima Mundi once more.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Hmm, not a bad job I guess. Now be on your way, I have meditating to do.

lines.speaker: Spirit Tree
lines.text: Tree whisperer, the Anima Mundi cries out in pain. Please, as a true friend of the spirit trees, find the source of this taint. May Guthix be with you.

sections.title: Talking to Longramble after healing the spirit tree

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: Hello there. Any luck with that outlet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I ended up getting a bit side-tracked thanks to that spirit tree over there. Where did you say the outlet was?

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: It's just to the west of here. Shouldn't be too hard to find.

sections.title: The Warped Depths
Peeking through the heavy door before finishing the cutscene
Talking to Hazelmere before finishing the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Phew, that was a close one! Thanks for saving me.

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 21
lines.text: So, how did you get this ropey old job then, Seventy Two?

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 72
lines.text: I think it's a punishment, Twenty One.

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 21
lines.text: What for?

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 72
lines.text: I fell asleep while guarding upstairs.

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 21
lines.text: You fell asleep? I thought the masters made it impossible for us to fall asleep.

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 72
lines.text: I dunno, maybe I'm defective. I blame the masters myself. Anyway, I got the choice, either take this job or go outside.

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 21
lines.text: Outside?! I don't even want to think about it!

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 21
lines.text: Still, at least you got to guard upstairs! Things are always happening up there. I've been down here since I was de-podded and nothing happens down here.

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 72
lines.text: You'd have thought the masters would make it impossible for us to get bored as well.

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 21
lines.text: You'd think so, but they always seem to be making a mess of the pods. After all, you've seen how many failures get dumped outside.

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 72
lines.text: True.

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 72
lines.text: Wait! Did you hear something?

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 21
lines.text: Calm down, nothing ever-

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 72
lines.text: There's someone outside! What do we do?

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 21
lines.text: Press the red button! Quickly!

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 72
lines.text: They told us to never press the red button!

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 21
lines.text: Unless it's an emergency! Press the red button. Now!

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 72
lines.text: Don't panic!

lines.speaker: Terrorbird Guard 21
lines.text: I'm not panicking, you're panicking! Press the red button!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This might be a problem...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yup, that's a problem!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh good. Now I'm stuck!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Gas as well? Wonderful!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Feeling dizzy... I can't think straight...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is it. This is the end.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Help! Someone, help me! I'm dying...

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: What are you doing, stupid human?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you Death? You remind me a lot of a gnome I knew when I was alive.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: You're not dead yet, fool! Snap out of it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hazelmere?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Pull yourself together! We haven't got much time.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Argento's last gift to me. I was saving it for a special occasion. I suppose saving your hide counts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wow, that's some trick!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Megal!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: It seems I have made my choice. So be it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did you say?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Nothing you need to worry about now. We need to get out of here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Phew, that was a close one! Thanks for saving me.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Consider it a thank you for helping that spirit tree. You have shown a great empathy towards the Anima Mundi. Despite being a human, perhaps you could one day be a true spirit tree mage.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on... you're properly talking to me, without using a mind meld. I thought you only spoke ancient gnomish?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Bah! I've always been able to speak your uncivilised tongue.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Normally I wouldn't lower myself to these rudimentary words, but given your recent near-death experience, I felt you would not have the energy for a mind meld.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's unusually kind for you. Not going soft, are you?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Perhaps, but it seems I would be wise to ensure your safety. The future is not a place for old wizards like me.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: My autumn is ending, and only winter remains. Soon, it will fall to individuals like you to protect the Anima Mundi.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: The Anima Mundi granted me a glimpse into the future. I had one final choice to make, and I made it by saving you. We now walk a path that we cannot stray from, so listen closely...

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Skilled brothers, numbering eight, will assemble to fight the Mahjarrat deceiver, and they will be heading northwards to confront him.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: The time after this is... clouded to me, but I see you clearly, dressed in the evil garb of the darkest night.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: However, while you may dress in the colours of evil, you will not follow that path. You will remain true to Guthix.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Of the eight, only two shall return. One represents the path of the slayer and one the path of the warrior.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Things might have been different, but I have made my choice, and it is up to you to prove that I made the right one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like you have been having some bad dreams, or you've been reading some terrible stories.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Dreams often reveal truths masked by the light of day, but I know the truth of what I have seen in my bones. I have seen my end.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you can't die! You're a mighty wizard!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: All I know is what the trees tell me. It will soon be time for me to pass the guardianship of the spirit trees to someone else. Maybe that person is you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Guardianship of the spirit trees? I wouldn't even know where to begin with that!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: I have faith that you will learn what you need. Anyway, enough moping about the future. You should be on your way.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But what about Glouphrie?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Considering you nearly died trying to find him, I suggest you leave that problem for another day. Preferably after a better way has been found into Arposandra.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Now, I'm sure you have more important matters to attend to than chatting to a cranky old gnome. Take these and be on your way.

sections.title: Post-quest
Talking to Hazelmere to receive unused lamps if the player has sufficient inventory space

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: You're back. After these I'm guessing? Take them and stop disturbing me.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: You're back. After this I'm guessing? Take it and stop disturbing me.

sections.title: Talking to Hazelmere after the quest

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Hazelmere.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: You're back. Come to disturb me, have you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, good old Hazelmere. Glad to see you're back to your grumpy self.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Do you actually want something from me, or are you simply making my life a misery again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just wanted to have a chat. You know, just check how you're doing.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Well I'm fine, thank you. Now leave me in peace.

sections.title: Talking to King Bolren after the quest

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Hello there. Any luck in the quest for Arposandra?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sort of. I think I've worked out where Arposandra is, but my attempt to enter didn't go well. There seems to be quite an extensive set of defences.

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: Ah, that is a problem. I wish I could offer more help, but the Khazard Army already poses a major threat to us. We can't afford to split ourselves fighting two different enemies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So we do nothing?

lines.speaker: King Bolren
lines.text: For now, I'm afraid so. Glouphrie is a foe that must be dealt with one day, but that day is not today.

sections.title: Talking to Longramble after the quest

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: Hello there. How goes the hunt for Arposandra?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I've found an entrance to the city, but it's too well defended. There's all these traps, and some talking terrorbird guards!

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: Talking terrorbirds? There must be some strange magic being practised in there. What do you plan to do?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know. I think I've hit a dead end for now.

lines.speaker: Longramble
lines.text: That's a shame. Still, you've made a big discovery. It will be quite the tale to tell... assuming anyone believes a story about talking terrorbirds, that is.
